---
title: "Check Your Understanding"
id: "OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se01-sec03-sp03"
section_id: "OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se01-sec03"
session_id: "OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se01"
toc: True
---











<!-- IMPORT_START -->

Once we understand the structure of outbreak data, we can begin calculating meaningful measures such as vaccine effectiveness.

## The Concept Behind the Calculation

Vaccine effectiveness compares how risk differs between vaccinated and unvaccinated groups.

## The Formula

VE = (1 − Relative Risk) × 100

## Working Through the Calculation

::: {.callout-note collapse='true'}

Step 1 :: Identify the number of cases and total population in each group
Step 2 :: Calculate risk in each group
Step 3 :: Divide risks to obtain relative risk
Step 4 :: Convert to vaccine effectiveness
:::

## Applying to Our Example

::: {.callout-note}
In this outbreak, the vaccinated group has much lower risk, leading to a high effectiveness estimate
:::

## Check Your Logic

::: {.callout-tip}
**Self-check:** Why is relative risk calculated before vaccine effectiveness?

**Suggested answer**

Because VE is derived directly from relative risk
:::

<!-- IMPORT_END -->
_Add your primary content here. Use headings, lists, and images as needed._

---

<!-- START_NAVIGATION -->


```{=html}
<div class="custom-page-navigation">
    <div class="nav-prev">
        
        <a href="OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se01-sec03-sp02.qmd">
            <span class="nav-label">← Previous</span>
            <span class="nav-title">Worked Example</span>
        </a>
        
    </div>
    <div class="nav-next">
        
        <a href="../../se02/index.qmd">
            <span class="nav-label">Next →</span>
            <span class="nav-title">Interpretation and Communication</span>
        </a>
        
    </div>
</div>
```

<!-- END_NAVIGATION -->
